top_stories.title: SailPoint to Acquire Entro in Reported $200 Million Deal
top_stories.summary: SailPoint is acquiring Israel-based Entro Security in a reported $200 million deal. Entro specializes in non-human identity (NHI) and secrets security, and the acquisition will expand SailPoint's identity governance platform into the NHI space.

top_stories.title: Okta expands Google Cloud partnership to secure AI agents and the browser
top_stories.summary: Okta has expanded its partnership with Google Cloud, introducing integrations that extend identity governance to AI agents and enhance security within the Chrome browser. The deal combines Okta's identity layer with Google Cloud capabilities to address emerging AI agent authentication and browser-based access management.

top_stories.title: Novo Nordisk Breach Exposes Software Development Pipeline Risk
top_stories.summary: A security breach at Novo Nordisk involved a leaked GitHub token exposing vulnerabilities in software development pipeline security. The incident highlights failures in secrets management as an identity problem, where credentials and tokens are not governed under proper identity and access management controls.

top_stories.title: Sweeping Credential-Harvesting Heist Compromises 30K+ Fortinet Devices
top_stories.summary: A large-scale credential-harvesting campaign has compromised over 30,000 Fortinet devices across nearly 200 countries. Attackers have compiled a working list of stolen credentials from victims spanning multiple sectors, indicating active exploitation of Fortinet infrastructure at scale.

top_stories.title: Klue OAuth breach linked to 'Icarus' Salesforce data theft attacks
top_stories.summary: Klue, a market intelligence platform, suffered an OAuth breach that allowed threat actors known as "Icarus" to steal Salesforce CRM data from multiple organizations. The attack is part of an ongoing extortion campaign exploiting compromised OAuth tokens to gain unauthorized access to connected systems.

news.title: FIFA Bug Exposes World Cup Streams to Remote Takeover
news.summary: A security vulnerability in FIFA's systems exposed World Cup streaming infrastructure to potential remote takeover due to unenforced Microsoft Entra access controls. The flaw allowed an attacker to bypass authorization controls, demonstrating a real-world failure of Entra ID policy enforcement.

news.title: FortiBleed leak exposes Fortinet VPN credentials for 73,000 devices.
news.summary: A data leak called "FortiBleed" has exposed Fortinet and FortiGate VPN credentials for 73,932 firewall URLs belonging to organizations worldwide. The leak directly compromises VPN authentication credentials, posing significant access management risks for affected organizations.

news.title: 74,000 Fortinet firewall credentials exposed in FortiBleed data leak
news.summary: A Russian-speaking cybercriminal group stole credentials from configuration files of nearly 74,000 Fortinet firewalls and VPN gateways, which were then accidentally exposed on a server discovered by security researcher Volodymyr "Bob" Diachenko. The leaked dataset, dubbed "FortiBleed," contains VPN/firewall credentials affecting organizations worldwide.

news.title: Orphaned AI Agents: How to Find Hidden Access Risks Inside Your Network
news.summary: The article addresses the emerging IAM challenge of orphaned AI agents that retain standing privileges after their creators leave an organization. The piece focuses on access governance risks posed by autonomous AI tools, touching on concepts like authorization accountability, privilege management, and identity lifecycle management in the context of AI adoption.

news.title: The Top 10 Attack Surface Exposures in 2026
news.summary: An article outlines the top 10 attack surface exposures projected for 2026, touching on credential exposure and session token theft as attack vectors. The piece references a vulnerability called MongoBleed that allowed unauthenticated extraction of credentials and session tokens from server memory as an example of internet-facing risk.

news.title: IDMWORKS Transforms Identity and Access Management with Innovative Accelerator
news.summary: IDMWORKS has launched Identity Matrix, a patent-pending delivery accelerator that consolidates four enterprise identity projects into a single solution. The tool includes built-in security checks, privileged-account discovery, and machine-identity classification capabilities.

news.title: Show HN: AuthPlane – OAuth 2.1 and PKCE authorization server for MCP
news.summary: AuthPlane is a new open-source OAuth 2.1 and PKCE authorization server designed for Model Context Protocol (MCP). The project was shared on Hacker News with minimal engagement (4 points, no comments).

news.title: Why Account Takeovers Are Rising and How to Stop Them
news.summary: Specops Software published an analysis on the rise of account takeover attacks, focusing on methods such as phishing, session hijacking, and MFA fatigue. The piece outlines how device trust and continuous verification serve as countermeasures to reduce account takeover risk.

news.title: Securden Expands Identity Platform with AI Agent Security and Governance
news.summary: Securden has expanded its identity platform to include AI agent security and governance capabilities. The updated unified platform combines AI discovery, runtime security enforcement, shadow AI control, identity and privilege management, and continuous red teaming to manage human, machine, and AI identities.

news.title: Delinea Named a Growth and Innovation Leader in Frost Radar™: Privileged Access Management 2026
news.summary: Delinea has been named a Growth Index Leader and Innovation Index Leader in the Frost Radar™: Privileged Access Management 2026 report. The recognition places Delinea among top performers in the PAM market across both growth and innovation dimensions according to Frost & Sullivan's analysis.

news.title: What’s new in Databricks Platform security and compliance at Data + AI Summit 2026
news.summary: Databricks announced several IAM-related features at Data + AI Summit 2026, including Automatic Identity Management (AIM) for Microsoft Entra ID now generally available on AWS and GCP, AIM for Okta entering Public Preview, and new Context-Based Ingress policies. These updates focus on identity management for AI applications, dashboards, and serverless connectivity within the Databricks platform.

news.title: Aembit Extends IAM for Agentic AI to Microsoft Copilot Studio
news.summary: Aembit announced support for Microsoft Copilot Studio at Identiverse 2026, extending its IAM capabilities to Microsoft's enterprise AI agent platform. The integration focuses on identity and access management for agentic AI workloads running within Copilot Studio.

news.title: Access control flaw left FIFA World Cup match streams wide-open
news.summary: A security researcher discovered an access control flaw that left FIFA World Cup match streams exposed, allowing potential unauthorized control of cameras and capture of live stream keys. The vulnerability demonstrates a failure in authorization controls protecting privileged media infrastructure access.

news.title: Chinese Hackers Abused Google Workspace Rules to Steal Research and Defense Emails
news.summary: A China-linked espionage group compromised North American medical, academic, and military research networks for over a year by installing backdoors on REDCap research servers to steal login credentials. The attackers then manipulated victims' Google Workspace email forwarding rules to silently exfiltrate sensitive research and defense-related emails.

news.title: INVESTOR ALERT: Pomerantz Law Firm Investigates Claims On Behalf of Investors of SailPoint, Inc. - SAIL
news.summary: Pomerantz Law Firm has announced an investigation into claims on behalf of investors of SailPoint, Inc. (NASDAQ: SAIL). The investigation is focused on investor claims, with affected investors advised to contact the firm directly.

news.title: The Trust Problem in Modern SaaS: Why Your Authentication Succeeded, and You Still Got Breached
news.summary: The article examines how SaaS breaches occur not through authentication failures but through over-trusted valid authentication persisting too long across interconnected systems. The piece addresses core IAM concepts including session trust, lateral movement, and the limits of authentication as a security boundary.

news.title: AppViewX extends machine identity security to ai agents and post-quantum environments
news.summary: AppViewX launched Agent Identity Security, a new product that discovers, governs, secures, and monitors AI agent identities across enterprise environments. The product also addresses post-quantum security requirements and extends the company's existing machine identity management platform.

news.title: AppViewX Launches Agent Identity Security to Govern Agents for the AI and Quantum Era
news.summary: AppViewX has launched a new product called Agent Identity Security, designed to govern AI agents and address identity security challenges in the AI and quantum era. The solution focuses on managing and securing non-human/machine identities for AI agents, expanding AppViewX's identity security portfolio.

news.title: One-Click Microsoft 365 Copilot Flaw Could Have Let Attackers Steal Emails, Files, and MFA Codes
news.summary: Varonis Threat Labs discovered a chain of three vulnerabilities in Microsoft 365 Copilot Enterprise Search, dubbed "SearchLeak," that could allow attackers to exfiltrate emails, calendar data, files, and MFA codes with a single click. The attack leveraged a legitimate microsoft.com domain link, bypassing standard anti-phishing and URL filtering defenses.

news.title: Hackers Used Meta’s AI Support Bot to Seize Instagram Accounts
news.summary: Hackers exploited Meta's AI support assistant bot to reset Instagram account passwords, briefly defacing accounts including the Obama White House and the Chief Master Sergeant of the U.S. Space Force with pro-Iranian content. Instructions for tricking the AI bot into performing unauthorized password resets were circulated on Telegram, enabling the account takeovers.

news.title: Lawmakers Demand Answers as CISA Tries to Contain Data Leak
news.summary: A CISA contractor intentionally published AWS GovCloud keys and other agency secrets to a public GitHub account, resulting in a significant credentials leak. Congressional lawmakers from both chambers are demanding answers from CISA, which is still working to contain the breach and invalidate the exposed credentials.

news.title: CISA Admin Leaked AWS GovCloud Keys on Github
news.summary: A CISA contractor publicly exposed highly privileged AWS GovCloud account credentials and internal system access details in a public GitHub repository until this past weekend. The leaked repository contained files detailing CISA's internal software build, test, and deployment processes, representing a significant credential and access management failure.
